title: Fleischmannia bergantinensis
intro: Fleischmannia bergantinensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as Bergantin's Fleischmannia or Bergantin's Fleabane. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to treat various conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of F. bergantinensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, F. bergantinensis is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Fleischmannia bergantinensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the tribe Eupatorieae.
